### Post by lime4x4 on 2008-01-06
to display 1366X768 60hz. Here is a copy of the xorg file. What i'm i missing here oh i'm replacing the dell monitor

### Post by Hawk__0 on 2008-01-06
I'm not sure about this part, but in MY xorg.conf, I have this:
```
Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Default Screen"** 0 0**
        Inputdevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        Inputdevice     "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

```
and you have this:
```
Section "ServerLayout"
Identifier "Layout0"
Screen 0 "Screen0" **1440 0**
Screen 1 "Screen1" LeftOf "Screen0"
Screen 2 "Screen2" RightOf "Screen0"
InputDevice "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
InputDevice "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection
```

Also, I had a similar issue where I couldn't get the resolution I wanted.  I had to remove all the extra resolutions from the screen section.  This is what mine looks like:
```
Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen"
        Device          "Failsafe Device"
        Monitor         "Failsafe Monitor"
        Defaultdepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth   24
                Virtual 1792    1344
                Modes           **"1400x900@70"**
        EndSubSection
EndSection

```

I would try this, but [COLOR="Red"]first back up your xorg.conf file!![/COLOR]
to do this, use:
```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.bak
```
this way, if you run into any issues, you can access the command line still and simply copy the xorg.conf.bak, back to xorg.conf.

---

### Post by Hawk__0 on 2008-01-06
yes but change that monitors range as i said above.  delete all the other resolutions, but the one you wish to use.

### Post by Shay Stephens on 2008-01-08
I have a 1366x768 monitor too, a TV actually.  It was working briefly before I started playing with compiz-fusion.  A different video driver was loaded with one of my changes, and with it my native resolution disappeared too.  I have not dug into which driver I was using previously.  But you might want to try a different driver and see if you can get the native resolution.

I am running a suboptimal 1280x800 right now.

---
